old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-p2y5","title":"Border router: Core mesh-to-internet forwarding","description":"## Problem

The gateway app exists but only handles local interfaces (BLE/USB to mesh). The actual border router functionality — forwarding packets between mesh and internet — is not built.

## What exists
- LoRa L2 integration
- BLE interfaces (Meshtastic/MeshCore/LCI compat)
- CoAP server for local config
- RPL root config flag
- Announce ingestion

## What's missing (this epic)

### Core forwarding
- Mesh → Internet: packets from LoRa destined for off-mesh addresses
- Internet → Mesh: packets from upstream destined for mesh nodes
- RPL DODAG root: advertise prefix, accept DAOs, build routing table

### IPv6 integration
- Prefix delegation: get /64 from upstream (DHCPv6-PD or static)
- Prefix advertisement: RA to mesh with delegated prefix
- Routing: install routes for mesh nodes, forward bidirectionally

### IPv4 support (optional but practical)
- NAT64: translate IPv4 internet to IPv6 mesh
- DNS64: synthesize AAAA records for IPv4-only hosts
- Or: just require IPv6 upstream (simpler, less compatible)

## Architecture

```
Internet (IPv6 or IPv4)
        │
    [Backhaul]  ← WiFi, Ethernet, or cellular
        │
   ┌────┴────┐
   │ Border  │  ← This epic
   │ Router  │
   └────┬────┘
        │
    [LoRa L2]   ← Already exists
        │
   Mesh nodes
```

## Shared vs hardware-specific

### Shared (this epic)
- IPv6 forwarding logic
- RPL DODAG root implementation
- Prefix management
- Routing table for mesh nodes
- NAT64/DNS64 (if needed)

### Hardware-specific (separate beads)
- WiFi driver (ESP32, RPi)
- Ethernet driver (RPi, PoE gateways)
- Cellular modem (future)
- Platform-specific network config

## Dependencies

- RPL implementation (spec section 8)
- SCHC compression (already built)
- LoRa L2 (already built)

## Acceptance criteria

- Gateway receives IPv6 prefix from upstream
- Gateway advertises prefix to mesh via RPL DIO
- Mesh node can ping internet host (via gateway)
- Internet host can reach mesh node (via gateway)
- Works on at least one hardware target (Heltec V3 or RPi)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T20:36:11.41181Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T20:36:11.41181Z","labels":["gateway","networking","core"]}
new_value:
{"notes":"## Updated backhaul priority

Based on discussion, revised priority order:

### 1. Yggdrasil (global LICHEN backbone)
- Any LICHEN node can reach any other LICHEN node
- No RIR bureaucracy, cryptographic addressing
- See epic: project-LICHEN-zt3c

### 2. Tailscale/WireGuard (internet access)
- Works anywhere (behind NAT, IPv4-only)
- Mesh nodes reachable from your devices
- Easy setup

### 3. ULA (mesh-internal, always works)
- fd00:lichen::/32 + IID
- No config needed
- Baseline connectivity

### 4. DHCPv6-PD ("proper" IPv6)
- For enterprise/ISP deployments that have it
- Nice to have, not required

## Addressing summary

```
Local mesh:     fd00:lichen:<mesh>::<iid>  (ULA)
Global LICHEN:  02xx:...::<iid>            (Yggdrasil)
Internet:       via Tailscale/WireGuard    (overlay)
"Proper" IPv6:  via DHCPv6-PD             (if available)
```"}

new_value:
{"notes":"## Updated backhaul priority

Based on discussion, revised priority order:

### 1. Yggdrasil (global LICHEN backbone)
- Any LICHEN node can reach any other LICHEN node
- No RIR bureaucracy, cryptographic addressing
- See epic: project-LICHEN-zt3c

### 2. Tailscale/WireGuard (internet access)
- Works anywhere (behind NAT, IPv4-only)
- Mesh nodes reachable from your devices
- Easy setup

### 3. ULA (mesh-internal, always works)
- fd00:lichen::/32 + IID
- No config needed
- Baseline connectivity

### 4. DHCPv6-PD ("proper" IPv6)
- For enterprise/ISP deployments that have it
- Nice to have, not required

## Addressing summary

```
Local mesh:     fd00:lichen:<mesh>::<iid>  (ULA)
Global LICHEN:  02xx:...::<iid>            (Yggdrasil)
Internet:       via Tailscale/WireGuard    (overlay)
"Proper" IPv6:  via DHCPv6-PD             (if available)
```
## Addressing update: Drop ULA

Per Yggdrasil integration (project-LICHEN-zt3c), we're simplifying to:

### Node addresses
- fe80::<iid> — link-local (control plane)
- 02xx::<iid> — primary (derived from Ed25519, works everywhere)

### No more ULA
- Drop fd00:lichen::/32 prefix
- No prefix delegation needed for mesh-internal
- 02xx addresses work locally without Yggdrasil connectivity

### Gateway routing
```
if dst is link-local:
    forward via LoRa neighbor
elif dst is 02xx:
    if in_local_mesh(dst):
        forward via LoRa (RPL/gradient)
    else:
        forward via Yggdrasil
elif dst is internet (other):
    forward via Tailscale/backhaul
```

### Prefix delegation (p2y5.3) becomes optional
- Only needed for "real" internet IPv6 (GUA)
- Most deployments: Yggdrasil + Tailscale, no DHCPv6-PD needed
- Keep as option for enterprise deployments"}

new_value: Completed core forwarding logic in lichen-gateway + rpl/hybrid updates for mesh-to-internet (02xx routing, RPL root DAO support via Node, tests/vectors/interop). Fixed all lints from 3 review passes. All created children closed; pre-existing human-blocked deps noted for follow-up.
